summary: A fortified defensive structure with a cross-shaped interior layout and central watchtower. The building features stonebrick walls, a flat roof with crenellations, and is surrounded by a decorative stone block perimeter. The interior contains a cruciform passage system with a central chamber and four protruding bastions or gun emplacements.

shape.overall_form: Cross-shaped with central tower and four corner bastions
shape.symmetry: Four-fold rotational symmetry around central tower

rebuild_hints: Build main cross-shaped walls first using stonebrick, approximately 3-4 blocks tall
Construct central tower rising 2 blocks above main walls with tiered roof structure
Add crenellations (alternating slab and air blocks) around entire roof perimeter
Create four corner bastions protruding outward from cross arms
Place decorative gravel/stone block perimeter fence in grid pattern around building
Add torch or lantern on top of central watchtower
Ensure interior cross passage is hollow/accessible
Maintain symmetry across all four cardinal directions
uncertainties: Exact interior floor height and chamber depth not fully visible
Precise wall thickness varies by angle of view
Whether interior contains multiple floor levels or single hollow chamber
Exact material of roof crenellations (could be slab_stone or full blocks)
Interior floor material not clearly visible in overhead views
